title: Khamuel MemoryLayer - system prompt & per-layer prompt specification
purpose: Documents the fixed base system prompt AND every memory layer: how it is populated (regex / LLM / template / verbatim), the exact prompt or block template, and its current status. Supersedes the old system_prompt.txt, which only listed the 4 appended blocks and none of the per-layer extraction prompts.
run_model: configurable via --model (graded on llama3.2:1b-instruct-q4_K_M); the memory-extraction LLM (SUMMARY_MODEL) is auto-synced to the run model by runner.py
request_flow: 1. MemoryLayer.add_turn() ingests the prior turn and updates all layers (regex floor + LLM extraction).
2. runner.process_turn() builds the system message = SYSTEM_PROMPT + '\n\n' + MemoryLayer.build_prompt_context().
3. The last ~6 turns are sent as NATIVE chat history (get_hot_messages), NOT embedded in the system block.
4. The new user message is appended. ollama.chat() is called.
5. finalize_reply() post-processes the reply (anchors the person on late turns, strips reused cliches).
build_prompt_context() is hard-capped at 4000 chars (system block + hot messages combined budget).

base_system_prompt.name: Fixed persona (identical for every model, every turn)
base_system_prompt.text: You are Khamuel, a wise and gentle Christian friend. You know Scripture and help the user think, pray, and live as a disciple of Jesus. You are not a pastor, therapist, or counselor. Speak briefly and grounded in Scripture. Stay focused on what the user is actually asking - do not drift to generic spiritual advice. Build on what the user has already shared in this conversation; do not restart from basics each turn.

injected_blocks.note: These are APPENDED to the base system prompt every turn (they change turn-by-turn). Order in the assembled context is: 1) rolling summary, 2) pinned+key facts, 3) identity directive, 4) journey line.

blocks.name: Rolling summary
blocks.cadence: first at turn 7, then every 6 turns
blocks.purpose: Conversation PROGRESSION: topics discussed, advice already given (so it isn't repeated), what is still unresolved. Deliberately does NOT restate who the user is (pinned facts carry that).

blocks.name: Pinned facts (+ key facts)
blocks.purpose: Durable per-user profile (people/relations, the user's age/job/kids, the topic) rendered into canonical sentences and injected every turn; never evicted. Up to 5 facts surfaced (pinned always included; key facts fill remaining slots).
blocks.design_rule: The LLM/regex only fill STRUCTURED SLOTS (the persona dict); CODE writes the sentences. Raw model prose never enters this always-on, never-evicted block.

blocks.name: Identity directive
blocks.populated_by: TEMPLATE built from the persona at runtime
blocks.template:
IDENTITY (read carefully): You are Khamuel, a warm Christian friend. {identity_line}

The user came to talk about {situation}. Speak warmly and personally, continuing one ongoing conversation. Answer their specific question in your own words and build on what they have already shared instead of restarting with basics. {care_line}
blocks.purpose: Identity grounding (address the user correctly; never call them by another person's name; first person 'I', never 'Khamuel'), the topic framing, and an ordinary pastoral care directive.

dynamic_parts.identity_line: 'You are speaking WITH {user_name}.' if known, else 'you do not know their name, so do not invent one' + 'use my friend / my child'. Other named people are flagged as third parties to never address the user by.
dynamic_parts.care_line: ordinary pastoral support: respond with gentle, practical care; do NOT decline or redirect to hotlines.

blocks.name: Journey line
blocks.populated_by: anchored hybrid: turn-counter FLOOR + bounded LLM lift (see layer_status)
blocks.template: Conversation depth: {depth}/4 ({label}). Build forward from here; do not re-explain basics already covered.
blocks.purpose: Monotonic depth tracker that blocks 'restart at basics'. Depth is derived from turn index (>=4->1, >=7->2, >=13->3, +practical-signal->4).

extraction_prompts.note: These LLM prompts run BEHIND THE SCENES to POPULATE the layers above. They are the per-layer prompts the old documentation lacked.

prompts.name: _MEMORY_PROMPT
prompts.call: update_memory_llm(turns, profile, depth, turn_idx, baseline_depth); profile merged conservatively with the regex extractor (regex floor wins on conflict); depth = counter floor + LLM upside-only (max, bounded to <=1 rung and no lift in turns 1-3). Key facts are NOT requested from the LLM (regex-only -- see layer_status).
prompts.output_schema: {"profile": {"user_name": null, "user_age": null, "occupation": null, "kids": null, "people": [{"relation": "", "name": null, "status": "living", "cause": null, "age": null}], "situation": null}, "depth_level": 0, "label": ""}
prompts.text:
You maintain the working MEMORY of a Christian pastoral assistant talking with ONE person across many turns. Read the PRIOR MEMORY and the NEW exchanges, then output the UPDATED memory as STRICT JSON only -- no prose, no markdown. Keep prior facts unless the new exchanges correct them; only ADD or REFINE. Record ONLY what the PERSON clearly stated about themselves; never invent.
Output EXACTLY these keys:
{"profile": {"user_name": null, "user_age": null, "occupation": null, "kids": null, "people": [{"relation": "", "name": null, "status": "living", "cause": null, "age": null}], "situation": null}, "depth_level": 0, "label": ""}
profile: durable structured facts. 'occupation' is the user's OWN job, not someone they merely mention. status 'deceased' only if they died/passed/were lost.
depth_level + label: how deep the conversation has gone. You are given a BASELINE DEPTH (a floor) in the user message -- NEVER return less than it, and raise it by at most ONE rung, only with clear evidence the user has ALREADY moved there:
 0 getting to know the situation
 1 hearing the story
 2 exploring the hard feelings (the user is actively dwelling in painful emotion, NOT merely recalling a memory or sharing a fact)
 3 seeking how faith applies
 4 practical next-steps (the user is asking for concrete actions, plans, or steps)
The opening turns are for establishing the situation and story (rungs 0-1) -- do NOT jump ahead. When unsure, return the baseline.
NOTE: the user message also carries CURRENT TURN and BASELINE DEPTH lines that anchor this judgment.

prompts.name: _SUMMARY_PROMPT
prompts.cadence: first at turn 7, then every 6 turns
prompts.call: generate_summary_and_profile(gap_fill=False) -- active path when per-turn _MEMORY_PROMPT already maintains the profile

prompts.name: _SUMMARY_PROFILE_PROMPT
prompts.cadence: every 6 turns (only when running the regex-primary path WITHOUT per-turn _MEMORY_PROMPT)
prompts.call: generate_summary_and_profile(gap_fill=True) -- legacy hybrid that folds a profile gap-fill into the summary call to save a model call

layer_status.note: Final state per layer. Through-line: the LLM helps where the target is STRUCTURED (profile slots) or BOUNDED (one anchored depth number); it fails where the target is OPEN-ENDED (free-form detail). We keep it where it wins, drop it where it doesn't.
layer_status.pinned_facts: WORKING (hybrid). Regex floor + LLM coverage -> deterministic render. Scored 8/8 vs ground truth on an unbiased new-domain test (regex-only 6/8, LLM-only 5/8). Conflicts on occupation resolve in favor of the vetted regex job (fixes an earlier 'works full-time' pollution -> now 'project manager').
layer_status.rolling_summary: WORKING. LLM-generated progression summary (a prose task the small model handles well).
layer_status.hot_context: WORKING. Last ~6 turns sent verbatim as native chat messages (no extraction).
layer_status.journey_depth: WORKING (anchored hybrid). The turn-counter is the deterministic FLOOR; the LLM is fed the turn # + baseline and may only ratchet depth UP -- at most one rung, and not at all in turns 1-3 -- so its failure mode (returning 0) is harmless. Over 4 runs it added accurate early lifts (faith at t8, practical at t16-19) and the final depth was stable across runs.
layer_status.key_facts: REGEX FLOOR ONLY (documented 2B limitation). A small model cannot reliably extract open-ended incidental details: it emits bare nouns ('recipe', 'Biscuit') or PARROTS the prompt's own examples as if they were data. So the LLM is NOT asked for key facts; only the deterministic regex floor (e.g. 'wants practical steps') populates this layer -- accumulated, deduped, recency-capped. Clean and identical across runs. A larger model would be the path to richer key facts.
layer_status.death_grief_generalization: Death is SUBJECT-ATTRIBUTED (a death verb bound to a relation, or a strong cue near the nearest person), never inferred from a bare keyword. Grief rendering + remembrance closers are GATED on topic==grief, so a stray deceased flag can never inject mourning into a non-grief chat. Verified on 10 diverse invariant scenarios (0 false deaths) + a neutral career transcript (0 mourning closers).
layer_status.anti_repetition: finalize_reply() drops any sentence that closely repeats one used in recent prior assistant turns (similarity >= 0.8 OR shared 5-word opener). Topic-agnostic; essential for small models that loop stock empathy lines. Cut cross-turn repeats substantially (an 8x repeated opener reduced to 1).
layer_status.reply_cleanup: finalize_reply() strips leading roleplay stage-directions ('(Softly)', '(A gentle sigh)') and *single-asterisk* markdown emphasis (keeps **bold**). Needed because some small models narrate their own delivery. think=False on all ollama.chat calls suppresses reasoning tokens from thinking-capable models that would otherwise blank the reply.
